request.system:
You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability.

Direction: judge whether this event makes the contract MORE likely to resolve YES (= bullish), LESS likely to resolve YES (= bearish), or has no directional effect (= neutral). IMPORTANT: direction is always relative to YES resolution. If your one_line_why says the event 'increases odds' or 'raises probability', direction MUST be bullish. If it says 'decreases odds' or 'lowers probability', direction MUST be bearish. Do not contradict yourself.

Magnitude: high = materially changes probability, medium = notable but not decisive, low = incremental signal.

Timeline shift: does this event suggest resolution will come sooner or later than expected, or no change (none)?
request.user:
Contract title: Will the SEC approve a spot Ethereum ETF by May 31, 2024?
Resolution criteria: Resolves YES if any spot Ethereum ETF receives approval from the U.S. Securities
and Exchange Commission by May 31, 2024, 11:59 PM ET. Approval means an effective
SEC order permitting listing on NYSE Arca, Cboe BZX, or NASDAQ. Resolves NO otherwise.

Conditions:
  A: SEC OKs NYSE Arca ETH spot ETF listing — By May 31, 2024, 11:59 PM ET, the SEC issues an effective order permitting NYSE Arca to list any spot Ethereum ETF.
  B: SEC OKs Cboe BZX ETH spot ETF listing — By May 31, 2024, 11:59 PM ET, the SEC issues an effective order permitting Cboe BZX to list any spot Ethereum ETF.
  C: SEC OKs NASDAQ ETH spot ETF listing — By May 31, 2024, 11:59 PM ET, the SEC issues an effective order permitting NASDAQ to list any spot Ethereum ETF.
Settlement entities: ['U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission', 'Division of Trading and Markets', 'Division of Investment Management', 'BlackRock', 'Fidelity Investments', 'VanEck', 'Grayscale Investments', 'Bitwise Asset Management', 'Invesco', 'Franklin Templeton']

Record title: Joint Readout of Principals’ Meeting of UK and U.S. Authorities Regarding Central Counterparty Resolution
Record date: 2023-10-17
Record regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
Record topic: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
Record entities: ['Bank of England', 'Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System', 'Brian Sullivan', 'Commodity Futures Trading Commission', 'Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation', 'Federal Reserve', 'Securities and Exchange Commission']

response.one_line_why: Meeting concerns central counterparty resolution and financial stability; mentions SEC but provides no information bearing on spot-ETH ETF approvals.
